text_blocks.text:
Internship Report on Kifiya Financial Technology
Full Name: Mistire Daniel
College: College of Engineering
Department: Software Engineering Department
Year/Semester: 4th year 2nd semster
Internship Organization/Company: Kifiya Financial Technology
Internship Duration: From June 11 To September 10
Company Supervisor: Addis Yehasab
Submitted to: Inst Befekadu
Submission date: 16/09/2025

Chapter One: Introduction
1.1 Brief Background of the Company/Organization
Kifiya Financial Technology is a leading fintech company in Ethiopia, established in 2010 with the goal
of expanding access to affordable and innovative financial services. Over the years, Kifiya has pioneered
digital solutions that enable individuals, businesses, and institutions to participate more effectively in the
financial ecosystem. Through products such as digital credit and payments infrastructure, device and asset
financing, AgriTech solutions, and AI-driven platforms like Michu, Kifiya has become a driving force in
promoting financial inclusion and digital transformation in Ethiopia.
1.2 Purpose of the Internship
The purpose of this internship was to make us understand how the real-world works and functions as a
Software Engineer.. It provided an opportunity to gain first-hand exposure to the daily operations,
technologies, and workflows of a leading financial technology company.
1.3 Objectives of the Internship
The main objectives of the internship were:
● To gain practical experience in software engineering within a professional fintech environment.
● To develop technical, problem-solving, and teamwork skills through involvement in real-world
projects.
1.4 Projects Undertaken
During the internship, I was assigned to work on the Admin Portal of the Unified Interoperable Rural
Agent & Network Management Platform, a digital financial system designed to unify fragmented rural
financial services. The platform enables agents and merchants to perform a wide range of transactions
such as cash-in/cash-out, bill payments, mobile money, and merchant payments through a single
interoperable wallet, while also supporting offline transactions with NFC smart cards.
The Admin Portal I contributed to serves as the central management interface for different administrative
roles, including the platform admin, network manager, support agent, and finance admin. Through this
portal, administrators can manage agents and merchants, monitor transactions, configure services, and
oversee reporting and compliance. It is connected to the system’s microservices via a dedicated
Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) layer, which ensures efficient communication and tailored APIs for frontend
use.
2

1.5 Activities and Responsibilities on the Project
My responsibilities included:
● Assisting in the design and development of the Admin Portal, focusing on dashboards and
role-based interfaces for platform admins, network managers, support agents, and finance admins.
● Building and styling UI components to support workflows such as agent/merchant management,
transaction monitoring, service configuration, and reporting.
● Integrating frontend components with the Backend-for-Frontend (BFF) layer using Redux Toolkit
for state management and API communication.
● Writing clean, maintainable, and testable code in line with industry best practices.
Chapter Two: Organization Profile
2.1 History of the Company/Organization
Kifiya Financial Technology was founded in 2010 by Munir Duri, with the mission of democratizing
access to financial services across Ethiopia. Initially, the company focused on digitizing utility bill
payments through a Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) model, aiming to simplify transactions and increase
accessibility. Over time, Kifiya expanded its offerings to include digital credit platforms, device
financing, and AgriTech solutions, solidifying its position as a leader in Ethiopia's fintech sector. The
company's growth has been marked by strategic partnerships with governmental and non-governmental
organizations, as well as recognition for its innovative approach to financial inclusion.
2.2 Vision, Mission, and Core Values
● Vision:
To make a contribution to add value to the lives of people by making transactions simple,
affordable, and within reach.
● Mission:
To create an integrated, scalable technology-based services infrastructure that provides
customer-friendly payment services and enables access to financial and non-financial transaction
services.
● Core Values:
○ Innovation: Embracing new ideas and technologies to drive progress.
○ Inclusivity: Ensuring that financial services are accessible to all segments of society.
○ Integrity: Conducting business in an ethical and transparent manner.
○ Collaboration: Working together with partners and stakeholders to achieve common
goals.
○ Customer-Centricity: Prioritizing the needs and satisfaction of customers in all
endeavors.
3

2.3 Organizational Structure
Kifiya operates with a hierarchical organizational structure designed to foster efficiency and innovation.
At the helm is the Chief Executive Officer (CEO), Munir Duri, who oversees the company's strategic
direction. Reporting directly to the CEO are several key executives responsible for various departments:
● Chief of Products:
John Guchuhi leads the product development initiatives, focusing on creating and enhancing
digital solutions that meet customer needs.
● IT General Manager:
Habitamu Azezew manages the company's IT infrastructure, ensuring the reliability and security
of its technological systems.
● Head of Marketing Digital Lending:
Lewam Assefa oversees marketing strategies for digital lending products, aiming to increase
awareness and adoption among target audiences.
● Chief Impact Officer:
Hayat Abdulmalik is responsible for driving the company’s social impact agenda, ensuring that
Kifiya’s innovations contribute to financial inclusion and sustainable development.
● Chief Information Officer (CIO):
Alazar Negesu leads the information systems, data strategy, and ensures the integrity, security,
and performance of Kifiya’s technological environment.
● Chief of Staff:
Woyneab Habte manages internal coordination, strategic planning, and assists the CEO in
executing organizational initiatives.
This leadership team collaborates closely to drive Kifiya's mission and objectives, ensuring that the
company remains at the forefront of fintech innovation in Ethiopia.
2.4 Products and Services Offered
Kifiya offers a diverse range of products and services tailored to meet the needs of various customer
segments:
